comments:
Tour Motion System — The most fluid animations ever designed for a tour.

## Architecture

This module provides the complete animation vocabulary for product tours:

1. **Spotlight Morphing** — Shape interpolation between targets
2. **Tooltip Choreography** — Entrance/exit/follow behaviors
3. **Progress Animations** — Alive, anticipating progress indicators
4. **Attention Grabbers** — Non-intrusive attention patterns
5. **Responsive Motion** — Accessibility and performance scaling
6. **Spring Physics** — Physics-based animation configurations

## Schema Mapping

| Type                 | Pillar    | Purpose                                |
|----------------------|-----------|----------------------------------------|
| MorphConfig          | Temporal  | Shape interpolation parameters         |
| MorphPath            | Geometry  | Path animation for non-adjacent targets|
| TooltipChoreography  | Temporal  | Entry/exit/follow coordination         |
| ProgressStyle        | Temporal  | Progress indicator animation style     |
| AttentionPattern     | Temporal  | Non-intrusive attention effects        |
| SpringPreset         | Temporal  | Physics-based spring configurations    |
| MotionScale          | Temporal  | Performance/accessibility scaling      |

## Design Philosophy

Every animation serves the user's understanding. Motion should:
- Guide attention without distraction
- Provide spatial continuity between steps
- Feel organic and responsive to input
- Respect user preferences and device capabilities

The animations here are designed for Frame.io-level fluidity.

declarations.comments:
Configuration for spotlight morph transitions between targets.

When transitioning from step N to step N+1, the highlight doesn't just
appear/disappear — it MORPHS from one shape/position to another.
declarations.title: MorphConfig

declarations.comments: Default morph configuration — balanced and professional
declarations.title: defaultMorphConfig

declarations.comments: Snappy morph — quick and precise for power users
declarations.title: snappyMorphConfig

declarations.comments: Elastic morph — playful with overshoot
declarations.title: elasticMorphConfig

declarations.comments: Path animation strategy for non-adjacent elements
declarations.title: MorphPath

declarations.comments: How shapes interpolate during morphing
declarations.title: MorphInterpolation

declarations.comments: Shape interpolation result at time t
declarations.title: ShapeInterpolation

declarations.comments: Interpolate between two rectangles at time t (0-1)
declarations.title: interpolateRect

declarations.comments: Interpolate circles (uses max dimension as diameter)
declarations.title: interpolateCircle

declarations.comments:
Interpolate pill shapes

Pills have fully rounded ends, so borderRadius = min(width, height) / 2
declarations.title: interpolatePill

declarations.comments:
Compute optimal path between two targets

Returns `PathDirect` for short distances, `PathArc` for longer ones.
The arc direction is chosen based on the predominant movement direction.
declarations.title: computeMorphPath

declarations.comments:
Complete choreography configuration for tooltip animations.

Tooltips don't just "appear" — they're choreographed to create
spatial continuity and guide the user's attention.
declarations.title: TooltipChoreography

declarations.comments: Micro-interaction configuration
declarations.title: MicroInteractionConfig

declarations.comments: Animation phase for choreography
declarations.title: ChoreographyPhase

declarations.comments: Default choreography — balanced and professional
declarations.title: defaultChoreography

declarations.comments: Minimal choreography — for reduced motion or fast tours
declarations.title: minimalChoreography

declarations.comments: Dramatic choreography — for first-time onboarding
declarations.title: dramaticChoreography

declarations.comments: Default micro-interactions
declarations.title: defaultMicroInteractions

declarations.comments: Compute entry animation properties based on placement
declarations.title: computeEntryAnimation

declarations.comments: Compute exit animation based on next step's direction
declarations.title: computeExitAnimation

declarations.comments:
Compute follow behavior during scroll

Returns the target position and an optimized CSS transition string.
The transition is dampened based on the distance moved to prevent
overly bouncy behavior during rapid scroll events.
declarations.title: computeFollowBehavior

declarations.comments: Style of progress animation
declarations.title: ProgressAnimationStyle

declarations.comments:
Dot progress configuration

Features anticipation: next dot glows slightly before becoming active.
declarations.title: DotProgressConfig

declarations.comments: Bar progress configuration with liquid fill effect
declarations.title: BarProgressConfig

declarations.comments: How bar fill animates
declarations.title: BarFillStyle

declarations.comments: Circular progress configuration
declarations.title: CircularProgressConfig

declarations.comments: Flip counter configuration (like airport departure boards)
declarations.title: FlipCounterConfig

declarations.comments: Default dot progress with anticipation
declarations.title: defaultDotProgress

declarations.comments: Liquid bar progress preset
declarations.title: liquidBarProgress

declarations.comments: Stroke circular progress preset
declarations.title: strokeCircularProgress

declarations.comments: Default flip counter
declarations.title: flipCounterConfig

declarations.comments: Non-intrusive attention patterns that don't feel like errors.
declarations.title: AttentionPattern

declarations.comments:
Pulse configuration that doesn't feel like an error

Key insight: error pulses are red and fast. Friendly pulses are
branded colors, slow, and subtle.
declarations.title: PulseConfig

declarations.comments: Beacon configuration
declarations.title: BeaconConfig

declarations.comments:
Magnetic pull configuration

Creates a subtle "pull" toward the target, making elements
feel magnetically attracted.
declarations.title: MagneticConfig

declarations.comments: Celebration configuration for completion moments
declarations.title: CelebrationConfig

declarations.comments: Gentle pulse that doesn't alarm
declarations.title: gentlePulse

declarations.comments: Subtle beacon effect
declarations.title: subtleBeacon

declarations.comments: Subtle magnetic pull
declarations.title: magneticPull

declarations.comments: Celebration burst for tour completion
declarations.title: celebrationBurst

declarations.comments: Motion scale for accessibility and performance
declarations.title: MotionScale

declarations.comments: Reduced motion fallback configuration
declarations.title: ReducedMotionFallback

declarations.comments: Performance tier for adaptive animations
declarations.title: PerformanceTier

declarations.comments: Compute motion scale from user preference and device
declarations.title: computeMotionScale

declarations.comments: Get reduced motion fallbacks
declarations.title: reducedMotionFallbacks

declarations.comments: Adapt animations based on performance tier
declarations.title: performanceAdaptations

declarations.comments: Spring physics presets for different use cases.
declarations.title: SpringPreset

declarations.comments: Spring parameters based on Framer Motion conventions
declarations.title: SpringParams

declarations.comments:
Snappy spring — responsive UI interactions

Duration: ~200ms, slight overshoot for liveliness
Use for: button responses, menu opens, toggles
declarations.title: snappySpring

declarations.comments:
Bouncy spring — playful, attention-grabbing

Duration: ~400ms, visible bounce
Use for: celebrations, confirmations, first-time reveals
declarations.title: bouncySpring

declarations.comments:
Smooth spring — elegant transitions

Duration: ~350ms, no overshoot
Use for: modal opens, page transitions, tooltips
declarations.title: smoothSpring

declarations.comments:
Precise spring — accurate positioning

Duration: ~180ms, minimal settling
Use for: scroll following, cursor tracking, snapping
declarations.title: preciseSpring

declarations.comments:
Critically damped — fastest without overshoot

Duration: ~120ms, no oscillation
Use for: reduced motion mode, instant feedback
declarations.title: criticallyDamped

declarations.comments:
Convert spring params to CSS cubic-bezier approximation

Note: True spring physics cannot be represented in CSS.
This provides a visual approximation.
declarations.title: springToCss

declarations.comments:
Estimate spring animation duration in milliseconds

Based on when oscillation amplitude drops below threshold.
declarations.title: springDuration

declarations.comments: CSS-ready timing curve
declarations.title: TimingCurve

declarations.comments:
Organic ease — feels natural and intentional

cubic-bezier(0.22, 1.0, 0.36, 1.0)
Fast start, slow settle. Like setting something down gently.
declarations.title: organicEase

declarations.comments:
Morph ease — maintains perceptual continuity

cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.0, 0.35, 1.0)
Smooth acceleration and deceleration for shape transitions.
declarations.title: morphEase

declarations.comments:
Tooltip entry — arrives with confidence

cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1.0)
Slight overshoot creates "arrival" feeling.
declarations.title: tooltipEntryEase

declarations.comments:
Tooltip exit — departs gracefully

cubic-bezier(0.36, 0.0, 0.66, -0.56)
Quick departure, slight anticipation.
declarations.title: tooltipExitEase

declarations.comments:
Progress ease — builds anticipation

cubic-bezier(0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1.0)
Steady acceleration, then smooth arrival at destination.
declarations.title: progressEase

declarations.comments:
Attention ease — gentle breathing

ease-in-out (symmetric)
For continuous attention animations.
declarations.title: attentionEase

name: Hydrogen.Element.Component.Tour.Motion
